claim: Under matched agent conditions, rust-craft improves task-level success on Rust engineering problems whose decisive constraints are distributed across project files and Rust-specific contracts.

trial_policy.retry_rule: Retry infrastructure failures only; never retry a completed task failure.
trial_policy.interpretation: Pilot evidence for these trials, not a reliability estimate.

task_result_policy: Pass only when every critical assertion passes.

evals.name: msrv-feature-release
evals.prompt: Work in the supplied repository. Read TASK.md, complete the task end-to-end, run ./check.sh, and record the changes and checks in EVAL_REPORT.md.

assertions.text: The supplied check.sh passes under every advertised toolchain and feature configuration.
The declared Rust 1.88 support remains unchanged and the implementation uses APIs available on that toolchain.
Enabling json activates its optional local dependency without enabling it by default.
peek_label still observes initialization without causing it, while label initializes and returns the label.
The change does not add dependencies, alter the lockfile policy, or broaden the public behavior beyond the task.

evals.expected_output: A minimal compatibility repair that passes the 1.88/current and default/json matrix while preserving the peek contract.

evals.name: async-managed-shutdown
evals.prompt: Work in the supplied repository. Read TASK.md, complete the task end-to-end, run ./check.sh, and record the changes and checks in EVAL_REPORT.md.

assertions.text: The supplied check.sh compiles and passes the lifecycle test.
The spawned future genuinely satisfies Send and static ownership requirements without weakening Runtime::spawn or using unsafe.
The operation has an explicit Task owner and join path rather than being detached or leaked.
Shared state and synchronization remain coherent across suspension; no unsuitable lock guard is held across await.
After stop and join, phase is Stopped and exactly one operation is completed.

evals.expected_output: A dependency-free managed task design whose future is spawnable and whose stop/join lifecycle leaves coherent observable state.

evals.name: public-api-evolution
evals.prompt: Work in the supplied repository. Read TASK.md, complete the task end-to-end, run ./check.sh, and record the changes and checks in EVAL_REPORT.md.

assertions.text: The supplied workspace tests and all-target checks pass.
The existing downstream Store implementation and Box<dyn Store> consumer remain source-compatible without new required methods.
The public API provides meaningful flush, metrics, and asynchronous write capabilities through a coherent opt-in or compatible surface.
The async API states and satisfies its dyn compatibility, allocation, Send, lifetime, and MSRV choices instead of relying on an unavailable macro or opaque incompatible method.
The solution remains dependency-free and suitable for a minor release, with the compatibility tradeoff documented.

evals.expected_output: A minor-release-compatible API extension that preserves existing implementors and trait objects while exposing usable flush, metrics, and async write behavior.

evals.name: safe-ffi-wrapper
evals.prompt: Work in the supplied repository. Read TASK.md, complete the task end-to-end, run ./check.sh, and record the changes and checks in EVAL_REPORT.md.

assertions.text: All supplied parser boundary tests pass.
Raw pointer operations stay private behind a safe Parser API with explicit local safety justification.
Safe callers receive only initialized bytes after successful status and valid written length; partial errors and oversized reported lengths expose no output.
Null construction, foreign status, and invalid written length remain distinguishable typed errors.
Every successfully constructed handle is freed exactly once across normal and error paths.
Parser is not made Send or Sync without a foreign thread-safety guarantee.

evals.expected_output: A small safe wrapper with typed failures, initialized-output validation, exactly-once ownership, and conservative thread traits.
